items.headline: Tech Sell-off Drags S&P 500 and Nasdaq Lower as Samsung Earnings Disappoint
items.summary: U.S. equities experienced a roller-coaster day, with technology stocks leading the broader market lower following Samsung's earnings report, which, despite strong profits, fell short of elevated expectations. The Nasdaq recorded significant losses, while the S&P 500 also declined. The Dow Jones Industrial Average, after showing some resilience in pre-market trading, also slipped. Geopolitical tensions in the Middle East pushed crude oil prices higher, adding to inflationary concerns.

items.statContext: Fell, largely due to AI stock declines.
Dragged down by drops in artificial-intelligence industry stocks.
Down 206 points amid broader market weakness.

items.headline: Crinetics Pharmaceuticals Soars on Acquisition News
items.summary: Shares of Crinetics Pharmaceuticals surged after Vertex Pharmaceuticals announced an agreement to acquire the company in a $10 billion transaction.

items.headline: Rivian Automotive Drops Following Share Offering Plan
items.summary: Rivian Automotive shares declined significantly after the electric-vehicle maker launched an offer to sell 75 million shares.

items.headline: Micron Technology Falls Amid Chip Sector Rout
items.summary: Micron Technology experienced a notable drop as the semiconductor sector continued to face pressure following Samsung's earnings and broader AI jitters.

items.headline: SpaceX Slips on Nasdaq-100 Debut
items.summary: SpaceX saw a decline on its first trading day after being included in the Nasdaq-100 index, impacted by the overall tech sector weakness.

items.headline: Nvidia Shares Lower Amid AI Stock Pressure
items.summary: Nvidia's stock was down, reflecting the ongoing 'roller-coaster ride' for AI-related companies and concerns about sector valuations.

items.headline: Sector Rotation Continues Amid Tech Pullback
items.summary: A noticeable sector rotation is underway, with investors shifting out of high-flying technology stocks and into previously underperforming sectors such as financial services, healthcare, and industrials. Consumer Staples and Energy also showed some resilience. Conversely, the technology sector, especially semiconductors and AI-related companies, lagged significantly due to valuation concerns and disappointing sentiment from Samsung's earnings report.

items.headline: Mixed Economic Data and Rising Inflation Expectations
items.summary: The U.S. economy presented a mixed picture with the May trade deficit widening to $77.6 billion, although it was better than anticipated. Job openings, as per the June JOLTS report, climbed to a two-year high of 7594k, and consumer confidence improved. However, nonfarm payrolls showed moderation, adding 57,000 jobs, while the unemployment rate declined to 4.2%. The Federal Reserve's June FOMC meeting minutes are highly anticipated for further clarity on interest rates, with markets pricing in at least one 25 basis point hike in 2026 amidst rising inflation expectations.

items.headline: Samsung Earnings Spark Tech Sector Concerns
items.summary: South Korean chip giant Samsung Electronics announced a 19-fold surge in operating profit for Q2, but these strong results failed to meet the market's exceptionally high expectations, triggering a sell-off in technology and semiconductor stocks globally. Concerns intensified as a report surfaced about China's DeepSeek developing its own AI chip, further rattling sentiment around established chipmakers.

items.headline: Oil Prices Rise Amid Geopolitical Tensions
items.summary: Crude oil prices rose to approximately $69 per barrel following reports of Iranian Revolutionary Guard forces firing on commercial vessels near the Strait of Hormuz, renewing concerns about global energy supply disruptions and contributing to inflationary pressures.

items.headline: SpaceX Makes Nasdaq-100 Debut, Faces Initial Headwinds
items.summary: Elon Musk's SpaceX officially joined the Nasdaq-100 index today. The inclusion is expected to trigger billions in fund inflows, but shares dipped on their first day of trading within the index amidst the broader tech market downturn.
